mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index constitutes a persistent positive signal for near-term equity returns because expanding factory output precedes earnings surprises.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in INDPRO are not positive in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off re-pricing in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous upward movement of the federal funds rate and consumer price index amplifies monetary tightening conditions, reducing real corporate margins and increasing discount rates to drive negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal of month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not produce negative average 20-day returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of high unemployment, increases in housing starts signal imminent demand recovery through construction-led GDP acceleration, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, positive changes in HOUST do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales captures current household consumption momentum that directly feeds into top-line revenue surprises for consumer-facing S&P 500 constituents over the immediate 5-day window.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth does not associate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 52% of weeks.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate trades below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint amplifies equity risk premium compression from unconventional policy expectations, generating positive 20-day returns.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirm a demand-supply loop where employment income fuels output absorption, producing stronger positive 60-day equity returns than either signal alone.
failure_condition: The joint up-move signal in PAYEMS and INDPRO fails to outperform the single-series PAYEMS signal by at least 2 percentage points annualized over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating by more than 1.5 index points over three months signals impending monetary policy tightening that reduces real equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL has risen more than 1.5 index points over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.
